description: Update the telemetry documentation page to list all 11 new anonymous telemetry events added to the MCP server, maintaining the existing privacy-first tone and table-based format.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Update telemetry docs with new events
userStories.description: Expand the telemetry reference page to document all 11 new events alongside the existing server_started event, maintaining the same privacy-first tone and documentation structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The telemetry.mdx page lists all 15 events (4 existing + 11 new) organized by category
Each new event has a table showing its properties with Property, Example, and Description columns matching the existing format
The introductory text is updated to reflect the expanded event set while maintaining the privacy assurance
The 'What is collected?' section covers all event categories: server lifecycle, plugin lifecycle, permissions, search, settings, and updates
No plugin names, search queries, error messages, or version numbers appear in any example values
userStories.notes:
Files: - docs/content/docs/reference/telemetry.mdx (update with new events) Context: The current telemetry.mdx documents only the `server_started` event in a single Table component. It has sections: Why is telemetry collected?, What is collected?, How is identity handled?, How to opt out, Verifying what is sent, and Data handling. The existing Table format uses RetroUI components: ``` Property Example Description `version` `0.0.82` OpenTabs server version. ...
``` Changes: Restructure the 'What is collected?' section into subsections with h3 headings by category: ### Server lifecycle - `server_started` (existing table — keep as-is but note it's one of several events now) - `server_stopped` (already exists but wasn't documented — add table with: session_id, uptime_seconds, tool_calls_total, tool_errors_total, tool_error_rate_bucket, extension_was_connected, peak_concurrent_dispatches, plugin_load_failures) - `extension_connected` (already exists — add table with: session_id, time_to_connect_bucket, profile_count) - `dispatch_timed_out` (already exists — add table with: session_id, had_progress_updates, duration_bucket) ### Plugin lifecycle - `plugin_installed` — session_id (UUIDv4), source ('side_panel') - `plugin_removed` — session_id, source ('side_panel'), was_failed (false), plugin_source ('npm') - `plugin_updated` — session_id, source ('side_panel') - `plugin_reviewed` — session_id, permission_set ('auto'), review_source ('agent') - `plugin_install_failed` — session_id, source ('side_panel'), error_category ('npm_failure') ### Permissions - `permission_changed` — session_id, target ('plugin'), new_permission ('auto') - `skip_permissions_changed` — session_id, enabled (true) ### Search - `plugin_search` — session_id, source ('side_panel'), result_count_bucket ('6+') ### Settings - `plugin_configured` — session_id, source ('side_panel'), had_required_fields (true) ### Updates - `server_update_available` — session_id - `server_update_applied` — session_id Update the intro paragraph from: "A single event — `server_started` — is sent each time the MCP server starts." to something like: "Events are grouped into categories. Each event includes only anonymous metadata — no plugin names, no tool calls, no file paths, no browsing data." Also update the summary line: "That's it. No plugin names, no tool calls, no file paths, no environment variables, no error messages, no browsing data." to reflect the expanded set while keeping the same reassuring tone. Constraints: - Match the existing Table component format exactly — use the same className values - Keep the existing server_started table intact, just reorganize under a subsection heading - Example values in tables must be realistic but contain NO identifying information - Session IDs in examples should show a truncated UUID pattern like the existing version example - The page's lastUpdated frontmatter should be updated to the current date - Do NOT remove or modify the opt-out, identity, verification, or data handling sections — those are unchanged
